title: Aruteous Gunnay

'''Aruteous "Rute" Gunnay''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] member of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], serving as an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] and adviser to [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] and [[Settlement Officer/Legends|Settlement Officer]] [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]] during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]], Aruteous Gunnay was a expert on biochemical warfare employed by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Gunnay worked on the separatist station of [[Nod Kartha]]. After escaping a [[Battle of Nod Kartha|battle]] who concluded to the republic forces victory, Gunnay was transferred into [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] staff, becoming an adviser for the Viceroy who was a leading member of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name=":0" /> Accompanying the Council and other members of Gunray's entourage to [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] after the death of [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], the Separatist leadership holed up inside an old [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility/Legends|mining facility]] and used it as a base of operations. Following [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]' death on [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] the Confederacy's ally, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], contacted the Council and told them to accept his new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="ROTS" />

Upon Vader's [[Mission to Mustafar (Skywalker and Tano)/Legends|arrival]], the Sith sealed off all access points within the main chamber and ignited his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]; little did the Separatist leaders realize that Sidious was in fact [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Having played both sides against each other, Sidious' use of the Council had come to an end. Vader [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|made short work]] of the guards, droids, Council members and their aides, striking down Gunnay with a slash across the chest that ended his life. His body was later seen when Nute Gunray was cut down as well.<ref name="ROTS" />

==Equipment==
Gunnay wore a [[Clothing/Legends|command headdress]] with orange and brown robes.<ref name="TVD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aruteous Gunnay was portrayed by [[Colin Ware]], who also portrayed [[Lushros Dofine/Legends|Lushros Dofine]] in the film.<ref name="EchoBase">{{Blogspot|subdomain=echobasenews|url=2011/12/echo-base-news-interviewscolin-ware.html|name=EchoBaseNews.com|text=Echo Base News interviews Colin Ware}}</ref>

In the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]], four Neimoidians look the same as Aruteous Gunnay in the "Assassination to Mustafar" level.

Gunnay's name is a play on the name of Nute Gunray, and the origins seem to stem from the fact that the otherwise anonymous Neimoidian aide in tan robes is incorrectly referred to as Nute Gunray in ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''.<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=0735#14905348|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-05-29|time=12:33 PM|quote=Is Dressing a Galaxy considered canon? Specifically the Neimoidian name "Rute Gunnay"? Yep. In the ROTS Visual Dictionary, this character was misidentified as Nute Gunray. The Rute Gunnay name was created as a nod as to why two characters might get confused.}}</ref> This error is partially fixed in ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]'', where he is identified as "Rute Gunray". Whether or not this is meant to name him as a relative of Nute Gunray is unknown. In ''[[Dressing a Galaxy: The Costumes of Star Wars]]'', he is properly referred to as 'Rute Gunnay'.

[[Daultay Dofine/Legends|Daultay Dofine's]] facemask was reused for Aruteous Gunnay.

In [[2014]], the character's full name was revealed as Aruteous in [[Rogues Gallery (department)|''Rogues Gallery: The Devious Neimoidians Revealed'']], an article written by [[Tim Veekhoven]], [[Kevin Beentjes]] and [[Sander de Lange]] for the [[Star Wars Insider 147|147th issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine.<ref name="SWI">{{InsiderCite|147|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: The Devious Neimoidians Revealed}}</ref> The name was given under the request of [[Leland Chee]], as he wanted an actual name and not an anagram for Nute Gunray.<ref name=":0">{{Facebook|username=star-wars-sourcebooks|url=notes/star-wars-sourcebooks/creating-naming-the-devious-neimoidians/676129629103814|name=Star Wars Sourcebooks|date=Jan 19, 2014|archivefile=File:NeimoidiansFacebook.pdf}}</ref>
